description: BIMGUARD AI compliance ruleset for crevice corrosion risk assessment in MEP building services. Checks Critical Crevice Corrosion Temperature (CCT) adequacy of stainless steel grades against operating temperature, joint geometry class, and environment severity. The defining finding of CC-001: SS316 in a pool plant room scores 0.00 galvanic (GC-001) but 0.89 Critical on CC-001 — demonstrating that galvanic checking alone is insufficient.
standards_referenced: EN ISO 15329:2007 — Crevice corrosion testing, wetting classification T0-T5
ASTM G48 Method B — CCT determination for stainless steel grades
CIRIA C692 — Stainless steel in construction, CCT data table
CIBSE Guide G — Public health engineering, crevice corrosion guidance
IMOA Design Manual 4th Ed. — PREN formula and stainless grade selection
BS 8539 — Fixings in construction, bi-metallic assembly
ISO 19650 — BIM information management, IFC property set framework

mechanism_description.summary: Crevice corrosion initiates in geometrically confined spaces where restricted electrolyte circulation creates an aggressive local chemistry that destroys the passive film on stainless steel — the same passive film that makes these grades resistant to galvanic attack.

four_stage_process.stage_1_initiation: Confined electrolyte initially identical to bulk solution. Oxygen depletion begins as cathodic reduction consumes O2 faster than diffusion can replenish it.
four_stage_process.stage_2_differential_aeration: O2-depleted crevice becomes anodic. Metal dissolution begins: M -> M2+ + 2e-. Electrons travel to oxygenated exterior (cathodic).
four_stage_process.stage_3_autocatalytic: Metal cation hydrolysis lowers local pH. Chloride ions migrate in to maintain charge neutrality. Passive film destroyed by low pH and high Cl- concentration.
four_stage_process.stage_4_propagation: Autocatalytic chemistry is self-sustaining. Corrosion continues even if external conditions improve — until crevice is eliminated or pipe perforated.

scoring_model.formula: Score_CC = (0.35 x geometry_risk) + (0.40 x CCT_adequacy) + (0.25 x environment_severity)
scoring_model.rationale: CCT adequacy receives highest weight (0.40) as it is the most material-specific determinant — whether the specified grade can resist crevice attack at the operating temperature is the central question. Geometry (0.35) determines whether crevice conditions can establish. Environment severity (0.25) reflects chloride concentration and wetting frequency.
scoring_model.cct_adequacy_calculation: Linear interpolation: score 0.00 when operating temp is 20+ degrees below CCT (fully adequate). Score 0.60 when at CCT. Score 1.00 when 30+ degrees above CCT. Extrapolated linearly between these points.

Low.bcf_action: Asset register only — no BCF issue

Medium.bcf_action: BCF Normal priority — monitor and review joint specification

High.bcf_action: BCF Major priority — material or joint type upgrade required

Critical.bcf_action: BCF Critical priority — immediate material substitution or redesign

cct_table.description: Critical Crevice Corrosion Temperature for common MEP stainless grades. Below CCT = passive film stable. Above CCT = crevice attack initiates.
cct_table.source: ASTM G48 Method B / CIRIA C692 / Sandvik Corrosion Handbook
cct_table.test_conditions: 6% FeCl3 solution per ASTM G48 Method B

geometry_classes.description: Four geometry classes describing the degree of crevice restriction. Source: CIBSE Guide G / CIRIA C692.

Open.description: Butt welds, open pipe ends — free electrolyte circulation

Moderate.description: Socket welds, slip-on flanges, press fittings — some flow restriction

Tight.description: Weld neck flanges, compression fittings, lap joints — significant restriction

Critical.description: Threaded connections, under clamps, gasket contact — maximum crevice conditions

joint_type_library.description: 14-type joint library mapping IFC element types and joint descriptions to geometry class. JT-014 (unknown) defaults to Tight — conservative fallback when joint data is absent from IFC model.

JT-014.label: Unknown / unclassified
JT-014.note: Conservative default when joint type cannot be determined from IFC data

environment_severity.description: Seven severity classes per EN ISO 15329:2007 wetting classification framework.

high_risk_configurations.description: SS316 flanged connection in pool plant room
high_risk_configurations.academic_significance: GC-001 score 0.00 (Low) — this is the defining finding justifying the dual-engine architecture

high_risk_configurations.description: SS304 flanged connection in pool enclosure
high_risk_configurations.note: SS304 CCT = -5 degrees C — at risk in any above-freezing environment with chloride

high_risk_configurations.description: SS316 threaded connection in humid plant room above 40°C
high_risk_configurations.note: Threaded joint (Critical geometry) combined with operating above CCT

high_risk_configurations.description: SS304 compression fitting in normal indoor environment at 15°C
high_risk_configurations.note: SS304 CCT only -5°C — modest temperature still 20°C above CCT

high_risk_configurations.description: Super Duplex 2507 flanged in pool environment at 45°C
high_risk_configurations.note: CCT +50°C — only 5°C margin at 45°C operating temperature

mitigation_catalogue.MIT-CC-001: Upgrade stainless steel grade — specify Duplex 2205 (CCT +25°C) or Super Duplex 2507 (CCT +50°C) per CIRIA C692
mitigation_catalogue.MIT-CC-002: Change joint type to reduce geometry class — specify butt weld instead of flanged or threaded connection
mitigation_catalogue.MIT-CC-003: Specify PTFE or elastomer gaskets with minimum crevice geometry at flange interface
mitigation_catalogue.MIT-CC-004: Apply crevice-resistant surface treatment — electropolishing or passivation per ASTM A967
mitigation_catalogue.MIT-CC-005: Reduce chloride concentration in operating environment — improve ventilation or drainage
mitigation_catalogue.MIT-CC-006: Lower operating temperature below material CCT — review system temperature setpoints
mitigation_catalogue.MIT-CC-007: Specify titanium Grade 2 (CCT +120°C) for elements in permanent high-chloride high-temperature service
mitigation_catalogue.MIT-CC-008: Implement periodic inspection regime at identified crevice geometry locations

properties.name: CC001_JointTypeCode
properties.description: Joint type code JT-001 through JT-014

properties.name: CC001_CCT_C
properties.description: Critical Crevice Corrosion Temperature for specified grade

properties.name: CC001_EnvironmentKey
properties.description: T0_DRY through T5_IMMERSION per EN ISO 15329:2007
